headline: How Netflix uses VPC, Bastion Hosts & Security Groups to protect its streaming infrastructure
why: Netflix runs thousands of EC2 instances across multiple VPCs. Direct public access to internal servers would be a massive security risk — a breach could expose subscriber data or take down streaming for millions.

topics.usage: Each Netflix service (billing, recommendations, encoding) lives in its own VPC with a private CIDR range (e.g. 10.0.0.0/16). Services only talk to each other through controlled peering — not the public internet.

Engineers who need to SSH into internal EC2 nodes (for debugging or deployments) go through a single hardened Bastion Host. The bastion is the only machine with a public IP — everything else is private.

Each layer has its own Security Group: the bastion allows SSH only from the Netflix corporate IP range; the app servers allow traffic only from the load balancer SG; the DB servers allow traffic only from the app server SG.

The public subnet (where the load balancer lives) is attached to an Internet Gateway. Private subnets (app + DB) have no direct internet route — they exit through a NAT Gateway for outbound-only traffic like OS updates.

diagram:
graph TD
    Internet([🌐 Internet]) --> IGW[Internet Gateway]
    IGW --> LB[Load Balancer\nPublic Subnet\nSG: 443 open]
    LB --> APP[App Servers\nPrivate Subnet\nSG: from LB only]
    APP --> DB[RDS / DynamoDB\nPrivate Subnet\nSG: from App only]
    Engineer([👨‍💻 Engineer]) -->|SSH| Bastion[Bastion Host\nPublic Subnet\nSG: SSH from corp IP only]
    Bastion -->|SSH| APP
    style Internet fill:#1e293b,color:#94a3b8
    style Engineer fill:#1e293b,color:#94a3b8
    style Bastion fill:#7c3aed,color:#fff
    style LB fill:#1e3a5f,color:#dbeafe
    style APP fill:#1a3320,color:#86efac
    style DB fill:#450a0a,color:#fca5a5
takeaway: Netflix's VPC design means a compromised app server cannot reach the database directly, cannot SSH into other machines, and cannot be accessed from the internet — the blast radius of any breach is contained to one Security Group boundary.
